concerns.text: Task T009 targets OpenNeuro ds003813 for dMRI, but FR-001 explicitly mandates downloading from 'HCP' using 'HCP multimodal parcellation'. The task does not address the HCP source requirement, creating a gap in satisfying FR-001.

FR-002 requires preprocessing 'resting-state EEG recordings' (real data) with ICA and filtering. Task T011 implements simulation of EEG from structural graphs. There is no task to download, preprocess, or handle real EEG recordings as mandated by FR-002 and US-1 AC1.

FR-002 and US-1 AC1 require handling real EEG artifacts (ocular/muscle) via ICA. Task T012 applies ICA logic to *simulated* data. No task exists to implement artifact rejection for actual EEG recordings, leaving FR-002 unfulfilled.

SC-004 requires measuring the proportion of participants with complete 'dMRI and EEG preprocessing pipelines'. Task T012b calculates this for the simulated pipeline only. No task exists to track or report the success rate of the missing real EEG pipeline.

FR-004 mandates detecting avalanches by thresholding 'z-score normalization (global mean/std) to the EEG signal'. Task T015 implements this, but since the upstream tasks (T011) only produce simulated data, the task effectively covers a requirement (FR-004) that assumes real EEG input, creating a semantic gap in the implementation of the spec's intent.

The 'Null Result Protocol' in T029b checks for '<10 usable subjects' but relies on the simulated pipeline. The spec's Assumptions and FR-001/FR-002 imply a real-data pipeline. If the real-data pipeline (missing) fails to find subjects, this task does not cover that failure mode.
